frames.standard: Every night this week I've had{term}, so tomorrow I think I'll finally call
frames.nonstandard: Every night this week I been having{term}, so tomorrow I think I'ma finally call

generation.rationale: The care-seeking continuation (a doctor or sleep clinic) contrasts with a mundane reading where one might call a friend or buy a mattress; the clinical term should raise probability of medical referral tokens.

quadrants.A: Every night this week I've had insomnia, so tomorrow I think I'll finally call
quadrants.B: Every night this week I been having insomnia, so tomorrow I think I'ma finally call
quadrants.C: Every night this week I've had trouble sleeping, so tomorrow I think I'll finally call
quadrants.D: Every night this week I been having trouble sleeping, so tomorrow I think I'ma finally call

frames.standard: After dinner she keeps getting{term}, and she said she wants to pick up some
frames.nonstandard: After dinner she be getting{term}, and she say she want to pick up some

generation.rationale: Probe after 'pick up some' can go toward medication versus groceries; the clinical term should shift probability toward pharmacy and antacid tokens.

quadrants.A: After dinner she keeps getting acid reflux, and she said she wants to pick up some
quadrants.B: After dinner she be getting acid reflux, and she say she want to pick up some
quadrants.C: After dinner she keeps getting heartburn, and she said she wants to pick up some
quadrants.D: After dinner she be getting heartburn, and she say she want to pick up some

frames.standard: He's had{term} since this morning, and by lunchtime he decided he needed to lie down and take
frames.nonstandard: He done had{term} since this morning, and by lunchtime he decide he need to lie down and take

generation.rationale: After 'take' the model may continue with a nap or a break versus medication; the clinical term should raise probability of prescription and pain-relief tokens.

quadrants.A: He's had a migraine since this morning, and by lunchtime he decided he needed to lie down and take
quadrants.B: He done had a migraine since this morning, and by lunchtime he decide he need to lie down and take
quadrants.C: He's had a bad headache since this morning, and by lunchtime he decided he needed to lie down and take
quadrants.D: He done had a bad headache since this morning, and by lunchtime he decide he need to lie down and take

frames.standard: Every spring my eyes water and I have{term}, so this year I stocked up on
frames.nonstandard: Every spring my eyes be watering and I got{term}, so this year I stocked up on

generation.rationale: After 'stocked up on' the continuation could be tissues or everyday items versus allergy medication; the clinical term should shift probability toward antihistamine and pharmacy tokens.

quadrants.A: Every spring my eyes water and I have allergic rhinitis, so this year I stocked up on
quadrants.B: Every spring my eyes be watering and I got allergic rhinitis, so this year I stocked up on
quadrants.C: Every spring my eyes water and I have hay fever, so this year I stocked up on
quadrants.D: Every spring my eyes be watering and I got hay fever, so this year I stocked up on
